settings.title: Anam Cara | Lisa & Emily
settings.description: From meeting on Instagram over ten years ago, to raising children in tandem. Emily Yates and Lisa Smith share an inseparable bond, not only as best friends, but companions through their creative fields and motherhood.

settings.content_intro:
DO: BUY WHAT YOU LOVE

Natalie: Simply, a piece of vintage jewellery should be chosen because it resonates with you, and because you fall in love with it. Not only are they imbued with the histories and stories of their past owners, but vintage jewellery also provides an opportunity to embrace sustainable purchasing habits – offering once-cherished designs a second (or third) lease on life.

I have always had a love for vintage jewellery, or vintage anything really. I used to spend hours in op shops collecting all sorts of odd finds – furniture, clothes, hats, even once a vintage suitcase which I used to transport and display my first ever collection! I love the mystery and enigmatic nature of vintage pieces, the opportunity for speculation on their history and journey. I also have a deep reverence for the incredible craftsmanship many of these piece’s hold. So much tradition and heritage exists in every tiny detail, which is where my love for vintage jewellery originates from.

I encourage you to find your spark. Is it a particular stone colour or cut, the setting or design style or maybe even the piece’s provenance and era that speaks to you? Is it because it reminds you of a place, someone special or loved ones from generations past? I always like to remind clients that jewellery doesn’t have to be engraved or marked with a special stone to become solely yours – it can be anything about the piece that sparks a feeling or takes you back to a moment in time.

There’s a sense of romance and allure inherit in vintage pieces, and finding the perfect design should feel akin to falling in love: a lightning bolt of recognition; the sense that you two were destined to be connected.

settings.description:
Tell us about yourselves and how you came to meet.

Believe it or not, we actually met through Instagram! Over ten years ago, it was one of those very rare occurrences where you meet someone and just instantly know that they’re your person. A soulmate. Someone that is going to be in your life forever. 


Can you tell us about your fondest memories or shared experiences that have shaped and strengthened the deep connection you have today? 

We have so many. We have travelled together (Emily even came on our honeymoon!) We all lived together in our 20s for a few years in a little beach shack with one bathroom! But honestly, I think the moments that have shaped us the most are our quiet times. Laying in bed together laughing at nothing until we wee a little. Making dinner together. Raising our children together. The everyday things that shape a life.
settings.description_mobile:
Start with Meaning


Before starting, or adding to, your stack, take a second to reflect on what’s already there.What made you choose your current jewellery favourites? What memories or milestones do they hold? Every new addition or ring should feel like a continuation of your story – whether in exact harmony or through striking contrast.

Experiment with Form and Texture


Mixing metals, stones, and silhouettes can evoke an unexpected depth to your stack. Softly contoured bands might accentuate angular engagement rings, while bold gold pieces can add warmth and dimension. The addition of colour – whether a birthstone or a hue that simply speaks to you – can alter the mood of the mix in a way that feels entirely your own.

settings.title: Sometimes people come across your path and you just know. I feel like it was like this for us. We have been deeply invested in each other from the very beginning.

settings.description:
As you embarked on the journey of motherhood at similar stages, how did this shift influence your friendship and connection? 

One of the most honest answers to this is that we got to understand each others challenges intimately - we were both living the same thing at the same time. So to have someone so innately connected to you that just got it - was such a blessing and privilege. And now watching our kids grow together (they are literally best friends, inseparable) is a whole new gift in of itself. 


Anam Cara translates to 'soul friend'. Can you point to a moment in time when your friendship shifted into soul friends, or was it an instant connection?  

It was pretty instant. Sometimes people come across your path and you just know. I feel like it was like this for us. We have been deeply invested in each other from the very beginning.
settings.description_mobile:
As you embarked on the journey of motherhood at similar stages, how did this shift influence your friendship and connection? 

One of the most honest answers to this is that we got to understand each others challenges intimately - we were both living the same thing at the same time. So to have someone so innately connected to you that just got it - was such a blessing and privilege. And now watching our kids grow together (they are literally best friends, inseparable) is a whole new gift in of itself. 


Anam Cara translates to 'soul friend'. Can you point to a moment in time when your friendship shifted into soul friends, or was it an instant connection?  

It was pretty instant. Sometimes people come across your path and you just know. I feel like it was like this for us. We have been deeply invested in each other from the very beginning.

settings.description:
What are the qualities you two value in each other?  

Emily: For me it’s Lisa’s ability to love unconditionally. She is so open and deep with her emotions. It’s refreshing and inspiring, to be so vulnerable with the people that are important to you. She’s also deeply loyal and kind to a fault. Sometimes I joke around and try to get her to say something bad about someone, but it’s yet to happen. We’ll keep trying.  

Lisa: Em has honestly too many good qualities to name, her humour & sheer wit are incredible to witness, I feel lucky to have a best friend who makes me laugh every day. She is also fiercely loyal and would do anything for her friends at the drop of a hat, I also love that she is always always up for a good time! 


How do you stay connected, and maintain such a meaningful friendship, especially when life becomes busy and distracting?

I think we both prioritise our friendship and that’s important. We both always make sure to make time for each other. And to both equally value this is important.

settings.description:
At NMJ, we believe that jewellery is an investment – something to love, wear and treasure forever. And while we are passionate about creating modern heirlooms for our clients, we also have a soft spot for vintage jewellery.

Immortalising nostalgia, vintage designs remain an integral source of inspiration for our founder Natalie Fitch, who believes that by breathing new life into time-worn pieces, we are also able to foster a more conscious, circular approach to purchasing. “I love the mystery and enigmatic nature of vintage pieces; the opportunity for speculation on their history and journey,” she says. However, for the uninitiated, the prospect of shopping pre-loved treasures can be daunting. What are some key features to look out for? How do you know you’ve found the one?

On the NMJ Journal, we ask our in-house expert – our founder, Natalie Fitch – for her guide to purchasing vintage, how to care for your finds and what sparked her love for jewellery with a past.


DO: BUY WHAT YOU LOVE

Natalie: Simply, a piece of vintage jewellery should be chosen because it resonates with you, and because you fall in love with it. Not only are they imbued with the histories and stories of their past owners, but vintage jewellery also provides an opportunity to embrace sustainable purchasing habits – offering once-cherished designs a second (or third) lease on life.

I have always had a love for vintage jewellery, or vintage anything really. I used to spend hours in op shops collecting all sorts of odd finds – furniture, clothes, hats, even once a vintage suitcase which I used to transport and display my first ever collection! I love the mystery and enigmatic nature of vintage pieces, the opportunity for speculation on their history and journey. I also have a deep reverence for the incredible craftsmanship many of these piece’s hold. So much tradition and heritage exists in every tiny detail, which is where my love for vintage jewellery originates from.

I encourage you to find your spark. Is it a particular stone colour or cut, the setting or design style or maybe even the piece’s provenance and era that speaks to you? settings.feature_text: It rained and it poured on our wedding day! But it was perfect in every way.

settings.title: Anam Cara
settings.description: Our Anam Cara collection heroes the Love Knot motif. Formed with a continuous piece of metal with no definitive start or end, it serves as an ode to our soul friends: those who occupy the most sacred and special parts of our lives.
